2605.04993.title: Federated Learning for Early Prediction of EV Charging Demand
2605.04993.paper_summary: Accurate forecasting of electric vehicle (EV) charging demand is critical for grid stability, infrastructure planning, and real-time charging optimization. In this work, we study the problem of early prediction of charging demand, where the total energy of a session is estimated using only information available at plug-in time and during the first minutes of charging. This enables actionable decisions while the session is still in progress, which is of direct importance for EV network operators. We construct a session-level dataset from the Adaptive Charging Network (ACN), combining session metadata with early-window charging measurements, and derive tabular features capturing user intent, temporal patterns, and initial charging behavior. We focus on a single operational depot, Caltech, and model intra-depot heterogeneity through station-level client partitions while evaluating multiple model families in a federated learning (FL) setting. Our results show that federated models can approach centralized predictive performance while keeping data in-depot, enabling privacy-enhanced training across distributed charging infrastructures. Overall, we demonstrate that reliable demand estimates can be obtained early in the session with minimal data, and that FL provides a practical pathway toward scalable and privacy-aware analytics for EV charging networks. Code is available at https://github.com/Indigma-Innovations/federated-learning-ev-charging-demand.
2605.04993.paper_summary_zh: 準確預測電動車（EV）充電需求對於電網穩定性、基礎設施規劃和實時充電優化至關重要。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們研究了充電需求的早期預測問題，其中會話的總能量僅使用在插入時間和充電的前幾分鐘內可用的信息進行估算。<lb/>這使得在會話仍在進行中時能夠做出可行的決策，這對於EV網絡運營商具有直接的重要性。<lb/>我們從自適應充電網絡（ACN）構建了一個會話級數據集，將會話元數據與早期窗口充電測量結合起來，並衍生出捕捉用戶意圖、時間模式和初始充電行為的表格特徵。<lb/>我們專注於單一運營的倉庫，Caltech，並通過站級客戶分區來建模倉庫內的異質性，同時在聯邦學習（FL）環境中評估多個模型系列。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，聯邦模型可以接近集中式預測性能，同時保持數據在倉庫內，從而實現跨分佈式充電基礎設施的隱私增強訓練。<lb/>總體而言，我們展示了可以在會話早期以最小數據獲得可靠的需求估算，並且FL為EV充電網絡提供了可擴展和注重隱私的分析實用途徑。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/Indigma-Innovations/federated-learning-ev-charging-demand 獲得。
2605.04993.author: Vasilis Perifanis et.al.
2605.04993.authors: Vasilis Perifanis, Foteini Nikolaidou, Nikolaos Pavlidis, Panagiotis Thomakos, Andreas Sendros
